title: KSPT Curricular Command Center
goal: The DPT program's accreditation process required manually mapping every course to CAPTE standards across disconnected spreadsheets. Each mapping cycle took weeks and was error-prone — a compliance risk for the department.
process: Built a SvelteKit 2 frontend using Svelte 5's new reactivity model, designing the component architecture around the accreditation data model before writing any UI code. Integrated Gemini AI to suggest standard mappings from course descriptions, reducing the manual review workload. Firebase provided real-time sync and Google OAuth, eliminating the shared-spreadsheet access problem entirely.
result: Manual mapping time reduced by approximately 60%. Faculty now work from a shared live interface rather than emailed spreadsheet versions.

title: WebVPython WASM Runner
goal: The VPython physics simulation library required a local Python installation — which meant every classroom demo started with a 20-minute setup session. Students who fell behind during installation missed the actual lesson.
process: Compiled CPython to WebAssembly via Pyodide and sandboxed execution inside an iframe. The non-obvious decision was routing all module imports through a postMessage bridge between the parent page and the iframe context, rather than attempting direct DOM manipulation across the frame boundary — a constraint that turned out to be essential for security. Input validation runs before any code reaches the interpreter.
result: Physics simulations run directly in the browser. No installation. Deployed via Docker for the VPython project team.

title: Bluetooth Attendance
goal: Manual attendance consumed several minutes of every lab session — time that compounded across sections, instructors, and semesters with no shared record system.
process: Detected student devices by scanning for registered Bluetooth MAC addresses. The multi-threaded design kept the scan loop running independently from the CSV write loop, so no scan cycle was blocked waiting on disk I/O. Lock management was the critical detail: partial-write corruption of attendance records would be worse than no records at all.
result: Full-classroom attendance records in under 30 seconds, without interrupting the session.

title: Obesity Risk Prediction
goal: Build a reproducible machine learning pipeline that classifies obesity risk from self-reported behavioral data — and demonstrate that structured feature engineering matters more than algorithm selection.
process: Ran parallel Logistic Regression and Random Forest classifiers on 2,111 records after EDA revealed class imbalance and correlated dietary features. Engineering interaction features between activity level and caloric intake improved Random Forest recall by 8% — more than switching models would have. Validation used ROC curves and confusion matrices to communicate results in a health context, not just accuracy numbers.
result: Classification accuracy competitive with published baselines. The pipeline is fully reproducible in a public Colab notebook.

title: American Gun Violence Analysis
goal: Examine whether state-level socioeconomic factors — poverty rates, education levels, firearm ownership — correlate meaningfully with gun violence mortality rates, or whether the relationships are weaker than commonly assumed.
process: Applied linear regression in R across national datasets, computing R² values for each variable pair to assess correlation strength rather than relying on visual pattern-matching. Built data visualizations to communicate statistically significant findings to a non-specialist audience. The honest result required reporting where the model did not find strong correlation, not just where it did.
result: A data-grounded analysis of which socioeconomic variables show meaningful association with state-level gun mortality rates, with explicit acknowledgment of model limitations.

title: Budget Manager
goal: Build a personal finance tracker that runs entirely at the command line — no web services, no accounts — as a study in Rust's ownership model applied to real I/O and parsing problems.
process: Used Rust's type system to enforce financial record invariants at compile time rather than runtime: an expense without a category or amount cannot be constructed, not just validated. The reporting interface required designing a query model that stayed expressive without becoming a full query language.
result: A working personal finance tool and a practical understanding of Rust's borrow checker applied to file I/O patterns.

title: Password Manager Testing
descriptor: Selenium E2E suite with CI integration
goal: The collaborative password manager project had no automated tests. Manual QA before every merge was slow and inconsistently applied — especially for multi-user sharing scenarios that required coordinating two browser sessions simultaneously.
process: Wrote Selenium end-to-end tests covering create, share, and retrieve flows. The sharing test required orchestrating two concurrent browser sessions and synchronizing their state without sleep() calls, which would have made the suite brittle against slower CI environments. Integrated into a CI pipeline to block merges on failure.
result: Test coverage on all critical user flows. Merge-blocking CI eliminated a category of regressions that previously reached code review.

title: Portfolio Website
goal: Build a portfolio that reads like a printed monograph rather than a SaaS landing page — one that communicates craft through typographic restraint instead of animation spectacle.
process: Designed a complete token-based CSS system from scratch: Cormorant Garamond paired with Space Mono, a warm parchment palette derived from editorial print references, and an asymmetric named-area grid. No UI framework, no Tailwind, no design templates. Scroll reveals use IntersectionObserver rather than scroll listeners to avoid main-thread blocking.
result: A site with a consistent editorial voice and a CSS bundle under 3 kB. Deployed on Vercel.
